_meta.title: Texting Wrapped — Population Benchmarks (machine-readable)
_meta.description: Structured benchmark distributions for computing user-vs-population comparisons. Each metric notes its population, geography, confidence, and source key (see sources.json).
_meta.usage_note: These are the best publicly available benchmarks, not a clean nationally-representative 2026 dataset. Treat reply-latency and group-chat numbers as directional. Where you have your own product telemetry, prefer it over these and recompute percentiles from your own user base.

reply_latency.metric: Time from message notification to opening/acting on it (NOT compose-and-send time)
reply_latency.population: Android users, primarily messaging apps incl. WhatsApp; sample 84% male, Germany/Europe
caveats: Measures open/interaction delay, not true reply latency (reply will be longer).
European Android-only sample; do not treat as US iMessage baseline.
No relationship-type breakdown exists in the literature.

age_effect.description: Each additional year of age adds ~0.39 min of mean interaction delay

read_within_time_us_adults.note: % who READ (not reply) within window; aggregator data, low confidence for personal texting

dating_context.finding: Gen Z daters 50% more likely than Millennials to deliberately delay responding
dating_context.population: Hinge users globally, n=15000, surveyed Aug 2023

message_volume.distribution_shape: heavily right-skewed (mean >> median); a small share of young heavy-texters dominate the mean

all_us_adults.note: 2024 overall avg, methodology not fully disclosed

context.note: Lower bound; excludes iMessage blue-bubble and app messaging counted separately

leaving_on_read.metric: Unread/open response debt and on-read behavior

findings.stat: of all SMS get any response
findings.note: all SMS incl. commercial, not personal

findings.stat: overanalyzed someone's digital body language (read receipts etc.)

concentration.finding: For most people, half of their texts go to just 3-5 people (Ling)

leaving_on_read.data_gap: No peer-reviewed study quantifies typical number of open/unanswered threads per person.

participation_inequality_rule.name: 90-9-1 rule
participation_inequality_rule.description: 90% lurk, 9% contribute occasionally, 1% produce most content; power-law/Zipf
participation_inequality_rule.note: Documented in large public communities; likely OVERSTATES inequality for small private group chats but directionally consistent

caveats: WhatsApp Germany 2016; may not match US iMessage groups.

read_receipts.data_gap: No representative quantitative adoption-rate-by-age data exists. Strong product opportunity to measure from your own telemetry.

platform_defaults.selection_effect: iMessage users with receipts ON have actively chosen visibility; WhatsApp privacy-conscious users opt out

directional_observation.finding: Read receipts less common among Gen Z/Millennials; more common with parents/older relatives
